text.title: Design Debt Vs Design Bet Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Design Debt Vs Design Bet application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every design task MUST carry a class tag: `debt` OR `bet`. Untagged tasks are blocked from sprint commit.
rule.rationale: Backlog-classification discipline — anchors Design Debt Vs Design Bet rule `explicit-class-tag`.

rule.statement: Tasks tagged `debt` MUST cite a specific friction-map entry or UX-audit finding with metric impact. No source = the task is actually a bet in disguise.
rule.rationale: Friction-mapping research — anchors Design Debt Vs Design Bet rule `debt-needs-friction-source`.

rule.statement: Tasks tagged `bet` MUST cite a falsifiable hypothesis (form: 'if we ship X, metric Y will move ≥Z within W weeks'). No hypothesis = the task is actually debt in disguise or vanity.
rule.rationale: Falsifiability discipline — anchors Design Debt Vs Design Bet rule `bet-needs-falsifiable-hypothesis`.

rule.statement: Sprint MUST allocate ≤30% capacity to bets, ≥20% to debt. Outside envelope = explicit roadmap-level decision.
rule.rationale: Budget-discipline research — anchors Design Debt Vs Design Bet rule `budget-envelope-per-class`.

rule.statement: Each bet MUST have a kill criterion (metric + threshold + observation window). Bets without kill criteria run forever and turn into debt.
rule.rationale: Kill-criterion discipline — anchors Design Debt Vs Design Bet rule `kill-criterion-on-bet`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
